setting.location: Blackwood Manor, a secluded estate in the English countryside

victim.name: Lord Alistair Finch
victim.background: Known for his ruthless business practices, vast collection of rare artifacts often acquired through questionable means, and a penchant for blackmail.

culprit.name: Eleanor Vance
culprit.means: Access to a rare Belladonna extract from the manor's old apothecary and knowledge of its effects, gained through research.
culprit.motive: Revenge for Lord Finch's role in her family's financial ruin and the theft of a priceless family heirloom, the 'Serpent's Eye' emerald, which he recently acquired.
culprit.opportunity: As Lord Finch's personal secretary, she had unrestricted access to his study and was responsible for preparing his evening tea.
culprit.method: Poisoned Lord Finch's nightly chamomile tea with Belladonna extract, using a stronger herbal blend to mask the bitter taste.
culprit.alibi: Claims she was in her private study, preparing documents for the morning, and only left to deliver the tea to Lord Finch, then immediately returned. She states she heard nothing unusual.

suspects.name: Dr. Elias Thorne
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Long-time acquaintance, professional relationship, but also a victim of Finch's blackmail.
suspects.means: Medical knowledge, access to various substances (but not the specific Belladonna extract used).
suspects.motive: Lord Finch was threatening to expose Dr. Thorne's past medical malpractice if he didn't comply with a dubious health report.
suspects.alibi: Left Blackwood Manor at 9:45 PM after a routine check-up and drove directly home, arriving at 10:15 PM. His housekeeper can confirm his arrival and that he immediately retired.
suspects.personality: Nervous, meticulous, easily flustered, carries a heavy burden of guilt.

suspects.name: Captain Arthur Sterling
suspects.occupation: Retired Military Officer, Lord Finch's Security Consultant
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Professional, but also a former rival in artifact collecting and a victim of Finch's deceit.
suspects.means: Physically capable, owns a service revolver (not used). No knowledge or access to the specific poison used.
suspects.motive: Lord Finch recently outbid him on a rare ancient map, humiliating him publicly and costing him a significant sum. He also suspected Finch of cheating him in a previous deal.
suspects.opportunity: Was in the manor's soundproofed billiard room from 9:00 PM to 11:00 PM, playing billiards alone. The room's acoustics would prevent him from hearing anything from the study.
suspects.alibi: Confirmed by the butler, Mr. Davies, who saw him enter the billiard room and heard the distinct clack of billiard balls until 11:00 PM when he left for bed.
suspects.personality: Gruff, honorable, proud, easily offended, but adheres to a strict code of conduct.

suspects.name: Mrs. Beatrice Davies
suspects.relationship_to_victim: Long-serving employee (30 years), deeply loyal to the manor itself, but resentful of Finch's plans.
suspects.means: Access to the entire manor, but no knowledge of poisons or specific means for this method of murder.
suspects.motive: Lord Finch was planning to sell Blackwood Manor and retire abroad, which would leave Mrs. Davies jobless after 30 years of service, with no pension or prospects.
suspects.opportunity: Was in the servants' quarters, preparing for the morning, from 9:30 PM onwards. She claims she was tidying and heard nothing.
suspects.alibi: Confirmed by other staff members (kitchen maid, footman) who saw her in the servants' hall until 10:45 PM, then heard her moving about in her private room until late.
suspects.personality: Stoic, traditional, fiercely protective of the manor's legacy, outwardly calm but inwardly seething.

clues.description: A half-empty teacup on Lord Finch's desk, smelling faintly of a strong, unfamiliar herbal blend, not his usual chamomile.
A small, silver locket with the initials 'E.V.' engraved on it, found partially hidden under a rug near the study entrance.
clues.location: Lord Finch's Study - Near the door

clues.description: A half-smoked cigar butt, still faintly warm, found in the waste bin next to Lord Finch's desk. It's a brand known to be favored by Captain Sterling.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Captain Sterling had visited Lord Finch earlier in the evening (around 7 PM) to discuss security matters and smoked a cigar then. He left before the crime.

clues.description: A single, torn page from an antique botanical guide, detailing the properties and effects of Atropa Belladonna, found tucked inside a ledger in Eleanor Vance's office.
A tiny, empty glass vial, with residue that tests positive for Belladonna extract, found hidden in a loose floorboard in Eleanor Vance's private quarters.
clues.location: Eleanor Vance's Private Quarters - Loose Floorboard

clues.description: A blank prescription pad, bearing Dr. Elias Thorne's name and clinic address, found on a side table in Lord Finch's study.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Dr. Thorne had left it behind during his routine check-up earlier that evening. It has no connection to the murder.

clues.description: A faint, sweet, almost sickly-sweet scent lingers around a specific shelf in the manor's old, disused apothecary, where several unlabeled bottles are stored. One bottle is slightly ajar.

timeline.event: Captain Sterling meets Lord Finch in the study to discuss security.
Dr. Thorne conducts a routine check-up on Lord Finch, leaving his prescription pad behind.
Captain Sterling enters the billiard room to play alone.
Mrs. Davies begins tidying in the servants' quarters.
Eleanor Vance prepares Lord Finch's evening tea, adding the Belladonna poison.
Eleanor Vance delivers the poisoned tea to Lord Finch's study and lingers to ensure he drinks it.
Lord Finch succumbs to the poison.
Mrs. Davies retires to her private room in the servants' quarters.
Captain Sterling leaves the billiard room and retires for the night.
Eleanor Vance 'discovers' Lord Finch's body and raises the alarm.

hidden_backstory: Eleanor Vance, Lord Finch's personal secretary, harbored a deep-seated vendetta against him. Years ago, Finch had ruthlessly ruined her family's shipping business, driving her father to bankruptcy and despair, and subsequently acquired their most prized possession, the 'Serpent's Eye' emerald, through dubious means. When she discovered Finch planned to sell the manor and its contents, including her family's heirloom, she decided her moment for revenge had arrived. Knowing Finch's nightly routine, she researched poisons in the manor's forgotten library, settling on Belladonna from the old apothecary. On the night of the murder, she prepared his tea, adding the poison, and lingered in the study, ensuring he drank it before returning to her office to establish her false alibi, leaving her locket behind in her haste.
